topStories.title: Rupee slips 29 paise to 95.27, RBI steps in to arrest slide
topStories.summary: The Indian rupee weakened by 29 paise to close at 95.27 against the dollar on Tuesday, pressured by foreign investor outflows and high crude oil prices. The Reserve Bank of India's intervention helped contain further losses, with the rupee trading between 95.29 and 95.02. Market participants await the RBI's rate decision and currency communication on Friday.

topStories.title: Oyo parent gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650-cr public offer
topStories.summary: Oyo, the hospitality brand, has secured approval from the Securities and Exchange Board of India for its initial public offering. The company plans to raise ₹6,650 crore through this IPO. Oyo is expected to file its updated draft red herring prospectus in the coming weeks. This marks the company's third attempt to go public.

topStories.title: Bhoomi Habba returns to Bengaluru
topStories.summary: The 18th edition of  Visthar’s annual environment and sustainability festival will offer an array of events and stalls that closely align with its core theme: Restore, Reclaim – Community Paths to Climate Action

topStories.title: Southampton murder involving kirpan sparks anger against police response and fears of religious division
topStories.summary: Vickrum Digwa, 23, was sentenced on Monday to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 21 years for killing 18-year-old Henry Nowak in Southampton on December 3, 2025

topStories.title: South Korea rides AI wave, also overtakes India on m-cap chart
topStories.summary: South Korea has surpassed India to become the world's sixth-largest equity market. This shift is attributed to strong performance from South Korean semiconductor companies. These firms are benefiting from booming demand within the artificial intelligence sector. India's market has seen a decline, partly due to foreign investor selling amid geopolitical concerns. South Korean tech giants are leading the rally.

topStories.title: US Strikes Iran's Qeshm Islands As Tehran Fires Missiles At Kuwait, Bahrain
topStories.summary: The US said that it has conducted self-defense strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island.

topStories.title: NHPC offer plugs into green energy appeal, more suited for patient play
topStories.summary: NHPC's offer for sale is underway. Analysts suggest it offers long-term potential for patient investors. The government is divesting up to 6% of its stake. The offer opened for non-retail investors with strong demand. The floor price is set at ₹71 per share. This move aligns with global energy security focus and domestic clean energy infrastructure investments.

topStories.title: Nifty IT logs best run in a year at 4%; TCS, Infosys lead way
topStories.summary: Indian IT stocks surged for a third consecutive day. The Nifty IT index saw its largest single-day gain in a year. This rally is supported by improving global software sentiment and expanding enterprise AI adoption. Factors like rupee depreciation and strong orderbooks also contribute. Analysts anticipate further upward movement in the IT benchmark.

topStories.title: US stocks today: US stocks end modestly higher as AI zeal overcomes Middle East jitters
topStories.summary: US stocks ended mixed as AI-driven optimism supported markets but was offset by rising geopolitical tensions and oil prices. Small-cap and semiconductor stocks gained, while software lagged. Alphabet’s massive AI funding plan and Marvell’s rally highlighted ongoing AI momentum, even as inflation fears and rate hike concerns kept investors cautious.

india.title: Who is LowTierGod? Dale Wilson turns heads with rare court victory amid alleged doxxing dispute
india.summary: LowTierGod, whose real name is Dale Wilson, is trending after reportedly winning a California family court case without a lawyer. Wilson received a three-year restraining order against the mother of his child over alleged harassment and sharing of personal information. The court outcome sparked major discussion across social media, with fans and critics debating the surprising legal victory.

india.title: LowTierGod reportedly secures restraining order against mother of his child
india.summary: Fighting game streamer LowTierGod has reportedly secured a restraining order against the mother of his child following a civil court hearing. Despite attending without legal representation, the streamer convinced the judge of harassment and unwanted contact. The order's specifics remain undisclosed, but the ruling has sparked significant online discussion within the gaming community.

business.title: Rupee slips 29 paise to 95.27, RBI steps in to arrest slide
business.summary: The Indian rupee weakened by 29 paise to close at 95.27 against the dollar on Tuesday, pressured by foreign investor outflows and high crude oil prices. The Reserve Bank of India's intervention helped contain further losses, with the rupee trading between 95.29 and 95.02. Market participants await the RBI's rate decision and currency communication on Friday.

business.title: Oyo parent gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650-cr public offer
business.summary: Oyo, the hospitality brand, has secured approval from the Securities and Exchange Board of India for its initial public offering. The company plans to raise ₹6,650 crore through this IPO. Oyo is expected to file its updated draft red herring prospectus in the coming weeks. This marks the company's third attempt to go public.

business.title: South Korea rides AI wave, also overtakes India on m-cap chart
business.summary: South Korea has surpassed India to become the world's sixth-largest equity market. This shift is attributed to strong performance from South Korean semiconductor companies. These firms are benefiting from booming demand within the artificial intelligence sector. India's market has seen a decline, partly due to foreign investor selling amid geopolitical concerns. South Korean tech giants are leading the rally.

business.title: NHPC offer plugs into green energy appeal, more suited for patient play
business.summary: NHPC's offer for sale is underway. Analysts suggest it offers long-term potential for patient investors. The government is divesting up to 6% of its stake. The offer opened for non-retail investors with strong demand. The floor price is set at ₹71 per share. This move aligns with global energy security focus and domestic clean energy infrastructure investments.

business.title: Nifty IT logs best run in a year at 4%; TCS, Infosys lead way
business.summary: Indian IT stocks surged for a third consecutive day. The Nifty IT index saw its largest single-day gain in a year. This rally is supported by improving global software sentiment and expanding enterprise AI adoption. Factors like rupee depreciation and strong orderbooks also contribute. Analysts anticipate further upward movement in the IT benchmark.

business.title: US stocks today: US stocks end modestly higher as AI zeal overcomes Middle East jitters
business.summary: US stocks ended mixed as AI-driven optimism supported markets but was offset by rising geopolitical tensions and oil prices. Small-cap and semiconductor stocks gained, while software lagged. Alphabet’s massive AI funding plan and Marvell’s rally highlighted ongoing AI momentum, even as inflation fears and rate hike concerns kept investors cautious.
